title: Evangelion Platinum in Region 2 is it worth getting
display_title: Evangelion Platinum in Region 2 is it worth getting
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Platinum in Region 2; is it worth getting?             Quote Post by FrDougal9000 » Wed May 15, 2013 4:09 pm Sorry for doing so many bloody threads lately but this one is very important; I found out that the Platinum DVDs are being sold on Play.com for varying amounts of money and I'm wondering if I should get them. I'm asking this because they're Region 2 DVDs (I live in Ireland you see) and I heard that there's some sort of ghosting image problem that goes on; is this common with most R2 DVDs and should I still get the DVDs despite this problem? Any help would be very much appreciated, believe me!

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Wed May 15, 2013 5:32 pm Im got the Platinums, dont recall anything untoward in the picture, some info here: http://wiki.evageeks.org/Renewal_of_Evangelion The picture is improved over the original DVD release, the special features are legendarily crap. You get the directors cuts of 21-24 though If you want ooh shiny boxes then go for it, they pop up pretty regularly on Ebay and Amazon UK.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Wed May 15, 2013 6:04 pm It's the Platinum release. If you don't already own it, get it. Next best thing to the Renewal set itself.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Wed May 15, 2013 8:37 pm http://www.ebay.co.uk/sch/i.html?_from=R40&_sacat=0&_nkw=evangelion+platinum&LH_PrefLoc=1 Either splurge £70-90 on the box set or spend a bit of time getting the individual volume cheap like I did :)

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu May 16, 2013 7:57 pm I say buy it. I own it myself, and it's amazing.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri May 17, 2013 2:24 am Nothing wrong with R2 Platinum as far as I'm aware. (And I see my tin box is considered "rare" by Ebay - well, well.)

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Fri May 17, 2013 6:47 am Yes well, lots of things are labelled as 'rare' on ebay that are no such thing. That tin pops up far too often to be anything like rare.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri May 17, 2013 6:48 am Hence the quotes; the "well, well" was mild surprise.
